title: Parent Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Parent Support Groups 43 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Autism Spectrum Disorder Disabilities Issues Hispanic/Latino Community Mothers Perinatal Depression Substance Use Disorder Issues 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details 90BY30 PARENT AND COMMUNITY CAFÉS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a space for parents and caregivers where they can come together to share their strengths and learn from one another. Volunteer facilitators help to create a safe space for peer-led conversations. Classes are available virtually for anyone in Lane County; in person Cafés are in Eugene and Springfield areas. 1600 Millrace Drive Suite 307 Eugene, OR 97403 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers Hours: 3rd Thursday of the month during the school year 6pm-8pm Email (541) 346-7484 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AUTISM SOCIETY OF OREGON SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of virtual and in-person support groups. 9320 SW Barbur Boulevard Suite 240 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Anyone in the austism community, along with their parents, caregivers, and supporters Hours: Varies by group Email (503) 636-1676 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BABY BLUES CONNECTION PREGNANCY AND POSTPARTUM SUPPORT RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support for parents and birthing people coping with perinatal mood and anxiety disorders through connection, information and resources. Services include 24-hour phone support, parent to parent support groups online and in-person and partner support for family members who want to support someone coping with postpartum depression or anxiety. Resource packets may be emailed or mailed at no cost. PO Box 33128 Portland, OR 97292 Eligibility: Parents experiencing any kind of perinatal mood disorders and stress related to welcoming a new child, and their family members. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 557-8375 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAY AREA FIRST STEP INC RESOURCE DEPARTMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in space for social activities. Offers assistance with resume building, help to quit smoking, and access to support groups for substance use issues and parenting. 155 S Empire Boulevard Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Individuals and families with minor children in substance use disorder recovery Hours: Monday/Thursday 8am-8pm, Tuesday-Wednesday/Friday 8am-5pm, Saturday 1pm-4pm, Sunday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 756-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGEWAY HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers developmental therapies and supports including in-home occupational and speech therapy, social groups, classes, and tutoring. Also offers parent support groups, parent and child activity groups, parenting classes, and specialized information and referral. 555 E 15th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Families with children living with autism and related disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 345-0805 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGING COMMUNITIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parent-to-parent mentoring, training, workshops, events, parent meetings, virtual meeting spaces, support groups, and advocacy. 1017 N Riverside Avenue Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Families with children living with disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (458) 278-0697 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADIA HEALTH CASCADIA PLAZA HEALTH CENTER CASCADIA CENTRAL INTAKE AND REFERRAL LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an intake line for services such as case management, individual, family, group, geriatric and LGBTQ counseling, alcohol and drug treatment services, DUII and gambling addiction/treatment services, recovery housing services, sexual abuse counseling, domestic violence services, urgent mental health services, medication management, money management, outreach, crisis intervention, outpatient mental health services, and early intervention services. Provides about 1000 units in sites operating as licensed residential, permanent supported housing, and independent housing. 4212 SE Division Street Suites 100 and 200 Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 674-7777 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY BUILDING BLOCKS CHELSEA'S PLACE RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers voluntary parenting support. The agency partners with parents by offering information about parenting and child development, a supportive professional who will listen and offer feedback, fun activities to do with your young child, therapeutic classrooms for children, connections with community resources, family nights and parent leadership opportunities. Access to food, diapers and clothing available for families enrolled in the program. 2425 Lancaster Drive NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Families with minor children prenatal-age 5 Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 798-4744 130 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY BUILDING BLOCKS DORIS'S PLACE RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers voluntary parenting support. The agency partners with parents by offering information about parenting and child development, a supportive professional who will listen and offer feedback, fun activities to do with your young child, therapeutic classrooms for children, connections with community resources, family nights and parent leadership opportunities. Access to food, diapers and clothing available for families enrolled in the program. 955 East Santiam Stayton, OR 97383 Eligibility: Families with minor children prenatal-age 5 Hours: Varies by service (503) 566-2132 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY BUILDING BLOCKS GRACIE'S PLACE RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers voluntary parenting support. The agency partners with parents by offering information about parenting and child development, a supportive professional who will listen and offer feedback, fun activities to do with your young child, therapeutic classrooms for children, connections with community resources, family nights and parent leadership opportunities. Access to food, diapers and clothing available for families enrolled in the program. 1135 Edgewater Street NW Salem, OR 97304 Eligibility: Families with minor children prenatal-age 5 Hours: Varies by service (503) 566-2132 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY BUILDING BLOCKS HELEN'S PLACE RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers voluntary parenting support. The agency partners with parents by offering information about parenting and child development, a supportive professional who will listen and offer feedback, fun activities to do with your young child, therapeutic classrooms for children, connections with community resources, family nights and parent leadership opportunities. Access to food, diapers and clothing available for families enrolled in the program. 180 18th Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Families with minor children prenatal-age 5 Hours: Varies by service (503) 798-4744 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY BUILDING BLOCKS INDY PLACE RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers voluntary parenting support. The agency partners with parents by offering information about parenting and child development, a supportive professional who will listen and offer feedback, fun activities to do with your young child, therapeutic classrooms for children, connections with community resources, family nights and parent leadership opportunities. Access to food, diapers and clothing available for families enrolled in the program. 1601 Monmouth Street Suite #101 Independence, OR 97351 Eligibility: Families with minor children prenatal-age 5 Hours: Varies by service (503) 566-2132 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY BUILDING BLOCKS WOODBURN PLACE RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers voluntary parenting support. The agency partners with parents by offering information about parenting and child development, a supportive professional who will listen and offer feedback, fun activities to do with your young child, therapeutic classrooms for children, connections with community resources, family nights and parent leadership opportunities. Access to food, diapers and clothing available for families enrolled in the program. 950 N Boone Ferry Road Woodburn, OR 97071 Eligibility: Families with minor children prenatal-age 5 Hours: Varies by service (503) 566-2132 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY PARENT EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a way for parents and caregivers to learn discipline strategies and stregthen parent and child interactions. Provides a variety of services for children and their caregivers, including therapeutic early childhood education, home visits, parent education, respite care, and basic needs support. 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Varies by class Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JOSEPHINE COUNTY PARENT EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a way for parents and caregivers to learn discipline strategies and stregthen parent and child interactions. Provides a variety of services for children and their caregivers, including therapeutic early childhood education, home visits, parent education, respite care, and basic needs support. 214 W 4th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Varies by class Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIRST STEPS CENTER FOR AUTISM AND DISABILITIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support groups, social opportunities, events, workshops, and resources for individuals and families experiencing neurodivergence. Accepts volunteers. 228 S Main Avenue Warrenton, OR 97146 Eligibility: Teen Group: Neurodivergent teens, ages 13-19 Adult Group: Neurodivergent adults, age 18 and older Parents and Caregivers Group: Parents and caregivers of neurodivergent individuals Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Teen Group: Friday 7pm Adult Group: 3rd Thursday of the month 6pm Parents and Caregivers Group: 2nd Thursday of the month 6pm Email (503) 861-8104 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOUR RIVERS EARLY LEARNING AND PARENTING PARENTING RESOURCES AND SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Parenting support group offering resources, referrals, and evidence based parenting classes. Helps build strong relationships with children. 400 E Scenic Drive Suite 207 The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Parents or guardians Hours: Varies Email (541) 506-2234 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLADSTONE CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting materials, family events, and a free lending library. It also hosts a variety of classes and groups offered by the agencies within the center. The agencies it houses include the Gladstone Health & Wellness Center, pediatric health, emotional and dental services. Family Stepping Stones, Relief Nursery, Head Start and Early Head Start, Northwest Family services navigators, Clackamas Education Service District EI/ECSE program, LifeWorks Northwest family counseling, WIC, SMART, Clackamas Community College GED program, and the Gladstone School District kindergarten program. 18905 Portland Avenue Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Households with children from birth through kindergarten Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm, also evening hours vary (503) 496-3939 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GROWING TOGETHER FAMILY COMMUNITY CENTER PLAY GROUPS AND CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of early childhood education and play groups for children. Also offers classes for adult parenting skills. 310 SW H Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Varies by class. Most classes are for children ages 0-5 and their caregivers. Hours: Varies by class Email (541) 864-0776 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOMS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support groups for stay-at-home mothers. Local chapters are linked on the website. 208 Hewitt Drive Suite 103 #328 Waco, TX 76712 Eligibility: Mothers raising children who stay at home during the day Hours: Not applicable Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES ABC PARENT SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers parent support groups that provide family-strengthening and child abuse prevention services. 7820 NE Holman Street Suite B5 Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers seeking support Hours: Message Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Support Groups: Varies Email (503) 258-4568 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES ABC LISTOS PARA APRENDER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting trainings, educational home visits, parenting issues support groups, parenting materials, referrals, case management, and developmental screening. Offers parents support with guidance on how to deal with a child's tantrums, questions about a child's developmental stages, and alternative types of discipline. 7820 NE Holman Street Suite B5 Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Spanish speaking parents with children ages 0-5 or who are pregnant Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, some evening appointments available Email (503) 258-4200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES ABC PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer mentoring to parents impacted by addiction who are involved in ODHS Child Welfare and criminal justice system. Parents are matched with a mentor who helps them stay on track, work through child welfare system and complete substance abuse treatment. Mentors help connect parents to recovery communities. The program is a part of Parents Anonymous Oregon. 7820 NE Holman Street Suite B5 Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Available to any parent who: -Has an open Child Welfare case due to substance abuse -Is currently in treatment for substance abuse -Is reuniting with their children after successful treatment Hours: Message line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 258-4590 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES SALEM PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer mentoring to parents impacted by addiction who are involved in ODHS Child Welfare and criminal justice system. Parents are matched with a mentor who helps them stay on track, work through child welfare system and complete substance abuse treatment. Mentors help connect parents to recovery communities. The program is a part of Parents Anonymous Oregon. 3857 Wolverine Street NE Suite 22 Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Available to any parent who: -Has an open Child Welfare case due to substance abuse -Is currently in treatment for substance abuse -Is reuniting with their children after successful treatment Hours: Message line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 258-4685 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER PARENTING GROUPS AND CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides childbirth education, newborn care classes, safe sleep workshop, breastfeeding workshop/support, and other classes which vary by season. 1515 NE 41st Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Most classes are for parents and other caregivers of children under the age of 5 Hours: Class times vary Email (503) 249-5801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS CENTRAL OREGON NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support groups. The groups are facilitated by trained volunteers with a focus on LGBTQ2+ individuals, veterans, families, and parents. PO Box 7462 Bend, OR 977087462 Eligibility: Individuals with mental health conditions and their families, support groups may have additional requirements Hours: Varies by support group Email (541) 316-0167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS CLACKAMAS NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support groups. The groups are facilitated by trained volunteers with a focus on LGBTQ2+ individuals, veterans, families, and parents. 10202 SE 32nd Avenue Suite 501 Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Individuals with mental health conditions and their families, support groups may have additional requirements Hours: Varies by support group Email (503) 344-5050 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS MULTNOMAH NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support groups. The groups are facilitated by trained volunteers with a focus on LGBTQ2+ individuals, veterans, families, and parents. Eligibility: Individuals with mental health conditions and their families, support groups may have additional requirements Hours: Varies by support group Email (503) 228-5692 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST NARA NW OUTPATIENT TREATMENT CENTER OUTPATIENT ADDICTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental assessments and evaluation, substance abuce counseling, parenting groups, gambling addiction treatment, DUI rehiabiliation programs, and traditional ceremonies. 1631 SW Columbia Street Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Age 18 and older. Provides culturally appropriate services for the Native American community. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 307-7775 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR YOUTH FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services, including counseling, support groups, violence and anger prevention training, life skills groups, parenting classes, new parenting groups, crime victims support, community information and referral and temporary housing. 965 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Ages 6-21 and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 386-6665 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST PARENTING PARENT TRAINING AND INFORMATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers parenting education programs, workshops, child development classes, support groups, parent–child activity groups, educational materials, and specialized information and referral. Divorce, custody, and court-approved classes are available on the website. 5825 NE Ray Circle Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 614-3188 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHSU CENTER FOR WOMEN'S HEALTH CENTER FOR WOMEN’S HEALTH CHILDBIRTH AND PARENTING PREPARATION CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of prenatal, childbirth, newborn care, breastfeeding education, and infant safety classes. Also offers several support groups, such as a new moms group. 808 SW Campus Drive 7th Floor Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Varies Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 418-4500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OLD MILL CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nursery providing services for at-risk families that include therapeutic classrooms for toddlers and preschoolers, home visiting, outreach and parent support groups. Resource closet available with food, children's clothing, diapers, wipes, hygiene items and cleaning supplies. 1650 SW 45th Place Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 who are low-income, a single-parent, teen parent, experiencing mental health or drug/alcohol issues, victims of trauma or domestic violence, involvement with child welfare, homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (541) 757-8068 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK MID-OREGON REGION OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 4263 Commercial Street SE Suite 300 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK NORTH OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 7110 SW Fir Loop Suite 160 Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK SOUTH OREGON REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 72A Centennial Loop Suite 150 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON PARENTING EDUCATION COLLABORATIVE HUB RESOURCE DIRECTORY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Parenting hub network offers parenting resources, groups, workshops, and classes. Eligibility: Must be a parent or guardian Hours: Varies by service Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX PARENT PDXPARENT.COM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about parent support groups, children's playgroups, and family activities. Offers a community events calendar, and magazine as well. Website Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 460-2774 Visit Website More Details PROVOKING HOPE RECOVERY SUPPORT SERVICES ADDICTION RECOVERY SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer, one-on-one, and group support on topics such as recovery, relationships, stress, co-dependency, parenting, and grief. Also provides specialized information and referrals. 213 NE 10th Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (971) 228-2020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE BIRTH CENTER MOM AND ME SUPPORT GROUP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Weekly Mom and Me support group with midwives teams and students to discuss about what happens from birth through the first year of parenting, parenting challenges, and support around postpartum depression. 1453 Redwood Circle Grants Pass, OR 97527 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (541) 916-8333 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SELF ENHANCEMENT INC SELF ENHANCEMENT INC COMMUNITY & FAMILY PROGRAMS CHILD WELFARE ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides supportive services to stabilize families, keep children out of foster care, and work toward reunification. Services parenting classes, support groups, case management, advocacy with Child Welfare System, and resource navigation. 310 NE Failing Street Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Must be referred by DHS Child Welfare Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 285-0493 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH LANE SCHOOL DISTRICT THE EARLY LEARNING CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supporting families with children to be successful in school by providing, and connecting to programs, and services that advance early literacy, promote health, and development, and foster strong resilient relationships. Also offers parent-child interaction play-groups known as Peggy's Primary Connection. 1310 S 8th Street Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Varies. (Peggy's Primary Connection 0-5 years old) Hours: Varies by service (541) 942-3532 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH YAMHILL COUNTY HHS FAMILY AND YOUTH PROGRAMS PARENTING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Collaborative Problem Solving (CPS) parenting classes and support groups for parents and other caregivers of children with challenging behaviors. Groups are led by peer volunteers and are a drop-in basis, but call before attending to verify time and location. 420 NE Fifth Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers Hours: Multiple sessions are available at varying times and locations. Details available to callers when they preregister.
